name: Text
desc: Draws read-only text on screen. No interaction. Used for labels, debug values, pattern names, current step display — anything the user just reads.
code:
ImGui::Text("hello world");
ImGui::Text("step: %d", seq->current_step);   // printf formatting works
ImGui::TextDisabled("greyed out label");         // muted color
ImGui::TextWrapped("long text that wraps");      // auto wrap
note: Never pass a raw std::string to Text() — use .c_str() or the %s specifier: ImGui::Text("%s", name.c_str()).
alsoknow: TextColored(ImVec4, "..") lets you pass a color inline without push/pop
LabelText("label", "value") draws a label+value pair aligned to the current column

name: Button
desc: The most common widget. Returns true on the single frame you click it. Size is optional — omit for auto-size.
note: The ## trick is critical. ImGui uses the full label as both display text AND internal ID. Two buttons with the same visible label must use ## to give unique IDs or behavior breaks.
alsoknow: SmallButton() — same as Button but no frame padding, sits inline with text
InvisibleButton() — invisible hit area, useful for custom drawn controls

name: SameLine
desc: Tells ImGui to place the next widget on the same line instead of a new line. The main tool for horizontal layouts.
note: SameLine(offset, spacing) — first arg is absolute x position (0 = auto next to previous), second is pixel gap. You used SameLine(60,60) to align names — BeginTable is cleaner for that.
alsoknow: Calling SameLine() after a full-width widget like SliderInt does nothing visible
For step buttons, vary spacing arg to create group gaps: (j%4==3) ? 8.f : 3.f

name: SliderInt / SliderFloat
desc: Draggable slider bound directly to a variable via pointer. Writes to your data every frame the value changes. Returns true when changed.
note: DragFloat/DragInt is often better for DAW controls — no fixed visual range, just drag. Third arg is drag speed (value change per pixel).
alsoknow: CTRL+click on any slider opens a text input to type an exact value — built in for free
SliderInt with a hidden label: use ## and draw your own Text() before it

name: PushStyleColor
desc: Temporarily overrides a color for all widgets drawn between push and pop. Stack-based — everything between the calls gets that color.
note: Common targets: ImGuiCol_Button, ImGuiCol_ButtonHovered, ImGuiCol_ButtonActive, ImGuiCol_Text, ImGuiCol_ChildBg, ImGuiCol_Border. Full list in imgui.h around line 1500.
alsoknow: Always match push count with pop count — mismatches corrupt the style stack until restart
ImVec4 is (R, G, B, A) with values 0.0 to 1.0, not 0-255

name: PushStyleVar
desc: Temporarily overrides a sizing or shape value — rounding, padding, spacing. Same push/pop pattern as StyleColor but for geometry not color.
note: StyleVar takes either a float or ImVec2 depending on the variable. FrameRounding is float, ItemSpacing is ImVec2. Wrong type = compile error.
alsoknow: Set FrameRounding globally once in your theme setup instead of pushing/popping everywhere
ItemSpacing affects both X and Y gap — set Y to 0 to pack rows tightly

name: BeginChild
desc: Creates a scrollable sub-region inside a window with its own clipping. Content that overflows the height scrolls independently. Always call EndChild — no bool check needed.
note: Unlike Begin(), BeginChild() always needs EndChild() with no conditional. The bool arg is whether to draw a border, not a success check.
alsoknow: Width 0 = fill available width. Height 0 = fill remaining window height
ImGuiCol_ChildBg sets the background fill color
Pass ImGuiWindowFlags as 4th arg: e.g. ImGuiWindowFlags_NoScrollbar

name: BeginTable
desc: Grid layout with proper column alignment. Eliminates SameLine offset math for track rows. Each column gets its own space and can be fixed width or stretchy.
note: Unlike BeginChild, BeginTable returns bool — always wrap in if(). WidthFixed pins to pixels, WidthStretch fills remaining space.
alsoknow: TableSetupColumn must be called before any TableNextRow
Useful flags: ImGuiTableFlags_BordersInnerV, ImGuiTableFlags_RowBg
You can call TableSetColumnIndex in any order — don't have to go 0,1,2 sequentially

name: BeginPopupContextItem
desc: Right-click context menu that appears on the last drawn widget. You already use this for delete pattern. Add as many MenuItems as you want.
note: The ID string must be unique per item — same ## rule as buttons. BeginPopupContextItem hooks onto the widget drawn immediately before it.
alsoknow: MenuItem returns true on click — use it like a button
BeginPopupModal blocks all other input while open — good for destructive confirmations
CloseCurrentPopup() closes whatever popup is currently open

name: InputText
desc: A text input field. Works on a char buffer — not std::string directly. Use for renaming patterns, file paths, search boxes.
note: Make the buffer static or it resets every frame. InputText works on char arrays, not std::string — copy in, check return, copy back out.
alsoknow: InputTextFlags_EnterReturnsTrue is great for DAW — commit on enter, cancel on escape
InputInt / InputFloat are number-entry versions with up/down arrows

name: Checkbox
desc: A toggleable bool. Returns true the frame it changes. Alternative to buttons for mute/solo states.
note: Label appears to the right by default. Use ## to hide it while keeping a unique ID.
alsoknow: RadioButton() is the single-select version — good for pattern mode selection
Checkbox binds directly to bool — no need to check return value unless you want a side effect

name: Separator / Spacing / Dummy
desc: Visual breathing room. Separator draws a line. Spacing adds a gap. Dummy reserves exact pixel space invisibly.
code:
ImGui::Separator();            // horizontal rule

ImGui::Spacing();              // one line of vertical gap
ImGui::Spacing();              // stack for more

// Dummy — exact pixel space, draws nothing
ImGui::Dummy(ImVec2(0, 12.f)); // 12px vertical gap
ImGui::Dummy(ImVec2(8.f, 0));  // 8px horizontal spacer with SameLine
note: Dummy() is the most precise — use it when you need exact pixel control over spacing between sections.
alsoknow: SeparatorText("label") draws a labeled section divider — good for grouping controls
Indent() / Unindent() push content right — useful for sub-menus or grouped settings

name: GetWindowDrawList
desc: Direct drawing API — draw rectangles, circles, lines at exact pixel positions. Used for custom controls like a piano roll, waveform display, or VU meter.
note: GetCursorScreenPos() gives absolute screen coordinates. After custom drawing, call Dummy() with the same size to advance ImGui's layout cursor — otherwise the next widget overlaps.
alsoknow: IM_COL32(r,g,b,a) uses 0-255 values unlike ImVec4 which is 0.0-1.0
GetBackgroundDrawList() draws behind all windows — useful for a global grid
This is how you'd build a piano roll — a big Dummy() region with custom note rectangles on top
